cases.title: Crouse v. Northwest Georgia Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC sustained the complaint, ruling that a Session erred by prescreening and eliminating a deacon's nomination for ruling elder before the mandatory instruction and examination process required by BCO 24-1. The decision held that BCO 24-1's language is mandatory and that sessions cannot add a preliminary vetting step that bypasses the required sequence of instruction and examination.

cases.title: Presbyterian Church in America v. Presbytery of the Mississippi Valley

parties.complainant_or_appellant: The Presbyterian Church in America (via BCO 40-5 report by unnamed Petitioner)

cases.synopsis: The SJC ruled that the Presbytery of the Mississippi Valley erred in approving a Session's erasure of a member's name from the roll under BCO 38-4 after she pled not guilty to a charge of failing to submit to church government and pursued divorce contrary to the Session's counsel. The SJC found multiple constitutional errors including improper conflation of a not-guilty plea with refusal to submit, failure to consider whether the member would fulfill vows in another branch of the church, failure to notify the member of her erasure, and proceeding to a case without process after dropping formal charges.
